questions.question: What was LLaVA's "embarrassingly simple" replacement for the Q-Former?
options: A 2-layer MLP (1024 → 4096 → 4096) projecting ALL 576 patch tokens straight into the LLM input — no bottleneck, no multi-objective pretraining, direct LM loss
questions.explanation: The Q-Former's bottleneck lost information and required co-design with every backbone pairing. LLaVA traded token budget (576 tokens per image) for simplicity and directness — and became the most copied multimodal architecture on the planet.

questions.question: Where did LLaVA's 158k instruction turns come from?
options: Text-only GPT-4 was fed COCO captions and bounding-box data and asked to generate conversations, descriptions, and reasoning questions — no human annotation
The model annotated its own outputs
questions.explanation: The data insight matched the architecture insight in importance: synthetic visual-instruction data generated from textual image descriptions made the recipe reproducible by anyone — 8 A100s for one day, 50+ forks by late 2023.

questions.question: What happens in LLaVA's two training stages?
options: Both stages train everything end to end
Stage 1: freeze ViT and LLM, train only the MLP on 558k caption pairs (a few hours); Stage 2: unfreeze the LLM (full or LoRA) and tune on the 158k instruction turns
Stage 1 trains the ViT; stage 2 the tokenizer
questions.explanation: Stage 1 aligns ViT-space to LLM-space with no task-specific supervision; stage 2 teaches instruction following. The prompt format replaces an <image> placeholder with the 576 projected tokens.

questions.question: What is the cost of LLaVA's no-bottleneck choice, and why did it stop mattering?
options: 576 context tokens per image — significant in a 2048-token context (1472 left for text), a rounding error at 32k
questions.explanation: Q-Former's 32-vs-576 token win was decisive in the 2k-context era. As LLM contexts grew, the token tax shrank while the simplicity dividend (any ViT + any LLM + one MLP) kept paying.

questions.question: Why did the community converge on MLP projectors despite the Q-Former's efficiency?
options: The Q-Former's intermediate objectives lost information in the bottleneck and every backbone change forced a retrain — the MLP trains on the final task loss directly and swaps components freely
questions.explanation: "Simpler won" for two structural reasons: loss directness (no intermediate representation the LLM must decode) and modularity (change the LLM or encoder without an R&D project).
